title: viola biflora var. sitchensis
intro: Viola biflora var. sitchensis, also known as the Sitka pansy, is a perennial plant that belongs to the violet family (Violaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Viola biflora var. sitchensis include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
